item.title: Lachy Groom to back India startup Pronto at a $200M valuation, sources say
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: This round, should it occur, would double the house-help startup's valuation in a matter of weeks.

item.title: Cohere acquires, merges with Germany-based startup to create a ‘transatlantic AI powerhouse’
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Cohere, the Canada-based AI company that makes AI tools for businesses in regulated industries, announced Friday it would merge with Aleph Alpha, a German company that also builds AI systems for businesses and governments.

item.title: Two college kids raise a $5.1 million pre-seed to build an AI social network in iMessage
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Series, a social networking app that's grown popular on college campuses, announced a $5.1 million pre-seed round from some big names in tech.

item.title: Apple’s new CEO, and why Elon Musk wants to buy Cursor for $60B
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Kirsten Korosec, Anthony Ha, Sean O'Kane
item.description: A new era is on the way for Apple as Tim Cook plans to step down from his CEO role in September, handing the reins to hardware chief John Ternus.   Ternus may be inheriting one of the most durable businesses in tech, but he’s also stepping into a very different ecosystem than the one Cook spent decades shaping. The App […]

item.title: Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga joins stacked StrictlyVC SF lineup for April 30 event
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Surprise! StrictlyVC San Francisco, which will kick off this year’s events lineup for TechCrunch on April 30 at the Sentro Filipino Cultural Center, is getting a new addition to its increasingly stacked lineup of speakers. Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga will join the rest of the lineup to discuss operating at scale in the age of AI.

item.title: Tim Cook is stepping down. What happens to Apple now?
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Tim Cook plans to step down from his CEO role in September, handing the reins to hardware chief John Ternus. Ternus may be inheriting one of the most durable businesses in tech, but he’s also stepping into a very different ecosystemthan the one Cook spent decades shaping. The App Store’s 30% cut is under pressure, the behind-the-scenes power Apple once […]

item.title: Redwood Materials loses COO amid layoffs, restructuring
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Former Tesla executive Chris Lister is retiring, and at least three other VPs have left Redwood around the recent restructuring, TechCrunch has learned.

item.title: Bret Taylor’s Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Sierra, the AI customer service agent startup founded by technologist Bret Taylor, announced today that it has acquired the YC-backed French startup Fragment.

item.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

item.title: Don’t stop hiring humans — stop hiring the wrong humans, Artisan’s founder says
item.dc:creator: Maggie Nye, Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: Artisan may be known for their bold “Stop Hiring Humans” campaign but the reality is every founder needs to assemble the right team if they want to scale.

item.title: Era raises $11M to build a software platform for AI gadgets
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Era thinks that we will see many form factors of AI hardware, including glasses, rings, and pendants.

item.title: Grab a ticket today: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 kicks off in just a week in San Francisco
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco is in just a week. Now’s the time to grab yourself a ticket. Join VCs and founders at Sentro Filipino Cultural Center on April 30.

item.title: Another customer of troubled startup Delve suffered a big security incident
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: TechCrunch has confirmed that Delve was the compliance company that performed the security certifications for Context AI, the AI agent training startup that last week disclosed a security incident.

item.title: Beehiiv rolls out new creator tools, including webinars and customizable paywalls
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: The announcement is a clear sign the company is trying to become an all-in-one hub for creators, reducing the hassle of juggling various tools and services to run their businesses.

item.title: Fusion doesn’t have a normal startup timeline, and investors are fine with that
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant, Rebecca Bellan, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Fusion energy has been “20 years away” for decades, but has the science finally caught up? Private investment in fusion companies surged from $10 billion to $15 billion in just months, and the money is coming from places you wouldn’t expect.  On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Rebecca Bellan and guest host Tim De Chant sit down with Rachel Slaybaugh, general partner at DCVC, to break down why serious […]

item.title: How SpaceX preempted a $2B fundraise with a $60B buyout offer
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Cursor was on track to close a $2 billion funding round this week but chose to halt discussions after SpaceX offered a $10 billion "collaboration fee" and a path to a $60 billion acquisition.

item.title: From the stage to the future: Where are Startup Battlefield’s alumni now?
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: We wanted to show you what happens after the confetti falls. We checked in with some of our recent alumni, many of whom have sat down with us on Build Mode: The Founder Survival Guide, TechCrunch's podcast for founders at every stage.

item.title: Fusion power may not be sci-fi. Just ask the people who sunk $5B into it.
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Fusion energy has been “20 years away” for decades, but has the science finally caught up? Private investment in fusion companies surged from $10 billion to $15 billion in just months, and the money is coming from places you wouldn’t expect.  Watch as Rebecca Bellan and guest host Tim De Chant sit down with Rachel Slaybaugh, general partner at DCVC on this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast. The trio breaks […]
